title: Isplourrdacartha Estillo
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image=[[File:X-wing_26.jpg]] |name=Isplourrdacartha Estillo |homeworld=[[Eiattu 6]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 13|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 13]] |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 1|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 1]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair=Red-brown |feathers= |eyes=Dark brown |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=[[Uthorrferrell Cartha]] |partners=new |siblings=*[[Dullourr]] *[[Kassen]] *[[Harrandatha Estillo]] |children=new |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Time for blaster safety lesson #1... Pointing a blaster at me isn't safe.|Plourr Ilo|''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Family Ties]]''}} [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] '''Isplourrdacartha Estillo''' of [[Eiattu 6]], known as '''Plourr Ilo''' for short, was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] member of the royal family of her homeworld, the capital of [[Ado sector/Legends|Ado sector]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], who was exiled and became a warrior in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], before being called home to take up the reins of government among her people. ==Biography== {{Quote|Plourr, try to remember that we're supposed to keep a ''low profile''.''"
"''I'm not exactly ''little miss wallflower'', Wes, but I'll make you a deal. I won't beat on any of the ''resident scum'' unless they hit me first—or they pick on my ''friends''—or I ''feel'' like it!|[[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] and Plourr Ilo|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 9|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 9]]}} [[File:Plourr_Ilo_as_little_girl.jpg|246x246px|left|thumb|Plour as a little girl]] Plourr grew up as one of the three daughters of the reigning monarch, [[Uthorrferrell Cartha]], but under her father and grandfather, the monarchy was forced to concede much of its honor in their accommodation with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and when she was still a young girl, the royal family were overthrown by the Eiattuan aristocracy, who preferred to negotiate directly with the Empire on their own terms. The initial coup was bloodless, but a faction of the nobility subsequently had the dynasty extinguished with savage, sadistic violence. In the chaos, Plourr was the only member of the royal house to escape; although in fact, it was Plourr herself, and not the [[Priamsta]], who killed arguably the most important member of the ruling dynasty: her vicious, psychotic brother [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Harrandatha Estillo|Harran]], who had become [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] protégé, and who attempted to stop her escaping so he could watch her die. Not much is known about the first years of Plourr's exile, except that she seems to have picked up a great deal of engineering knowledge and some piloting skills. The muscular, shaven-headed mechanic who eventually found her way into the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] was hardly recognizable as a princess of ancient lineage. By the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], she was serving as a non-commissioned technician aboard [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar's]] flagship [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']], and after Endor, she transferred to fly [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]]; although her attitude caused her problems in her early units, her reputation as a tough, brilliant pilot led [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] to bring her into [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], where she quickly earned a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant's]] rank. Highly skilled in hand-to-hand combat, Plourr combined her great physical strength and athletic appearance with a rather short temper and an almost eternally suspicious attitude; especially towards incompetent [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] mechanics, many of whom fell victim to the combination of strength and mood. She also excelled in black humor, not passing the opportunity to let go of a wry comment or two, even in the heat of battle. A warrior, she took great pride in military action and preferred head-to-head combat to covert operations, even though her service with the Rogues meant that she had to participate in more undercover missions than the average fighter pilot. [[File:Plourr_ibitsam.jpg|250px|thumb|Plourr Ilo and fellow [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue]] [[Ibtisam]] shake hands.]] Understandably, given her upbringing, Plourr hated the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and everything that it stood for. Some months into her service with the Rogues, however, she was recalled to her homeworld, where the ruling council of the nobility, the Priamsta, hoped that she could become the figurehead of a unity regime, ending both the tyranny of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Leonia Tavira]] and the terrorism of the [[People's Liberation Battalion]], led by an [[Harrandatha Estillo (impostor)|impostor]] who claimed to be Prince Harran. With the help of the Rogues Plourr successfully exposed the false Harran as a fraud—and, moreover, an agent of Tavira's. Taking power as [[Princess of the Realm]] and [[Princess of the Realm|Empress Apparent-Heir]], Plourr subsequently remained on Eiattu to lead her people once the Imperials had been expelled, insisting on ruling with the cooperation of the people and not simply the Priamsta, and was reunited with her father's cousin, to whom she was betrothed to as a child, [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Rial Pernon]]. This did not, however, end her association with the Rogues: she brought Eiattuan starfighter forces to support the Rogues on [[Malrev IV]], and Wedge allowed her to return to serve with them when she was able to, as she did in helping to [[Mission to Corellia (Rogue Squadron)|retrieve]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] defector [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel's]] family on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] from the clutches of Imperial agent [[Ilir Post]]. However, her duties on Eiattu demanded much of her time, and with Rial by her side, she grew rapidly into the role of planetary leader. She even began to grow her hair out again. Her instincts in conflict situations were still often for direct military intervention, however, as was seen not just at [[Malrev incident|Malrev IV]], but later when she offered to lead her fleet to rescue the captured Princess [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], actually a disguised [[Winter Celchu|Winter]], who had been [[Operation on Eiattu|kidnapped]] by the pirate gang now led by the ousted Leonia Tavira. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Plourr_Ilo_helmet.jpg|175px|thumb|Plourr Ilo's pilot helmet.|left]] She was originally supposed to be [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]], but [[Mike Baron]] overlooked that part of the outline given to him by [[Michael A. Stackpole]]. [[Nrin Vakil]] was later created to fill in the gap. Plourr is described in dialogue in [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: The Warrior Princess|''The Warrior Princess'']] as Uthorrferrell Cartha's youngest daughter, but a panel in the comic seems to show her as the middle daughter, with a younger sibling even younger than Prince Harran; it is possible that one sister is absent in the photo and the younger child is a brother not directly mentioned in the text. Alternatively, the woman beside her father might actually be Plourr's eldest sister rather than her mother, who is nowhere mentioned in the text (and possibly was already dead). This would make the child sitting on her lap in fact a nephew or niece. ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Isplourrdacartha Estillo}} *[[X-Wing: Rogue Leader
